rulings.Case Title: EDUARDO PANIAGUA ET AL VS. MILESTONE FINANCIAL, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS MILESTONE, BEAR BRUIN, WILLIAM STUART, AND ZOE HAMILTON'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is DENIED. Multiple triable issues of fact exist regarding the nature of the loan, the enforceability of the waivers, and statute of limitations. Plaintiff to prepare an order in conformity with CCP 437c(g). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW PAUL KANGAS VS. ESTATE OF CHRISTINA Y.C. CHEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2023 line 2. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege in good faith facts, not conclusions, that Plaintiff was a tenant. Plaintiff shall further allege facts in support of each element of each cause of action. If Plaintiff is asserting any claims independent of being a tenant, Plaintiff must so clearly state. If all Plaintiff's causes of action arise out of purported tenancy and its improper termination, Plaintiff must allege facts supporting the existence of tenancy (as opposed to license, employment, etc). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ERENDIRA LEYVA VS. HUMBERTO D. RUIZ & GUADALUPE RUIZ LIVING TRUST ET AL

rulings.Case Title: ERENDIRA LEYVA VS. HUMBERTO D. RUIZ & GUADALUPE RUIZ LIVING TRUST ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is Sustained without leave to amend as to the cause of action violation of PC 496(c). Plaintiff fails to cite any authority for the proposition that this code section applies to leaseholds. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for fraud for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, in support of each element of this cause of action. All amendments must be in bold font. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 2710 SUTTER VENTURES, LLC ET AL VS. SEAN MILLIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Award Of Reasonable Attorneys Fees And Costs On Appeal
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANTS SEAN MILLIS AND MICHELLE MATTERA'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF REASONABLE ATTORNEYS FEES AND COSTS ON APPEAL is GRANTED. Defendant is entitled to recovery of attorney's fees in this action (which included a payer for unlawful detainer damages for each day defendants remain in possession) under both the original attorney's clause contained in the Lease and the changed fees clause contained in the CCP 827 Notice. Evidentiary objection to the declaration of Jak Marquez/exhibit thereto is overruled. Hearing Required to address the reasonableness of attorney's fees and hours expended (lodestar). =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TAI LONG II LLC VS. JOSHUA GREGG BELL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2023 line 5. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, TO ESTABLISH ADMISSIONS, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS is granted in part and moot in part. Motion to deem admissions admitted is moot. Responses provided. Motion is otherwise GRANTED. Defendant to provide complete verified responses without objections to Form Interrogatories - General (Set One), Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One), Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $610. If Defendant provides timely responses in accordance with this order, the sanctions shall be reduced to $60. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: FRIENDSHIP VILLAGE, INC. VS. SHITA W. TILAHUN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 9, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED without leave to amend. Operative notice is fatally defective. CCP 1161. Plaintiff's argument that CCP 1161 applies only to commercial properties is not supported by any authority. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
